page.title: Living Items Classification Workbench
page.description: First establish the classification system in read-only mode, then decide whether to add data entry and management later.

overview.currentFocusDesc: These aren't random thoughts, but reminders emerging from system gaps, action rhythm, and purchase judgment.
overview.noSpotlights: No system reminders to highlight under current filters.
overview.actionRhythmDesc: Every need has a different next step: restock, wait for a deal, buy once, or leave it alone.
overview.purchaseAlertsDesc: View depreciation, slow-buy items, and essentials together for quick switching.
overview.noHighDepreciationWarnings: No high-depreciation happiness items that need extra caution under current filters.
overview.noSlowDepreciationSuggestions: No slow-depreciation long-term item suggestions under current filters.
overview.noEssentialsReminder: No essentials reminder under current filter.
overview.stagePulseDescription: The configuration focus that best represents your current life stage.
overview.systemPulseDescription: Which item systems are forming real support surfaces.
overview.spacePulseDescription: Space scenes reveal the areas you use and maintain most frequently.
overview.heroTitle: Treat shopping as life structure, not a to-buy list
overview.heroDescription: The overview brings items, stages, systems, and spaces into one page so you can judge what needs filling, where density is too high, and what supports your current lifestyle.
overview.curatedSubtitle: Put supporting items, spotlights, and collections in the same field of view.
overview.focusReasonFallback: When there are no spotlight hints, this becomes the main narrative area for shopping strategy and attention points.
overview.lifestyleFallback: Grouping items that serve one lifestyle makes your real usage scenes visible faster.
overview.structureSubtitle: More items is not always better. The key is whether they form clear layers.
overview.structureChartHint: See quantity and organization cost together

system.answer: Which life function does it support?
system.detail: Systems like sleep, diet, cleaning, work/study help you see gaps first.
system.cue: Identify which life gap it fills before deciding if it needs immediate attention.

space.detail: Bedroom, desk, kitchen, entryway — inspect by physical space.
space.cue: Place it back in real space to more easily spot missing items and duplicates.

stage.answer: Does your current stage need it?
stage.detail: Moving essentials, renting, long-term living, home ownership — different depths.
stage.cue: Match items to your current living stage; don't hoard for the next stage too early.

necessity.answer: Would things fall apart without it?
necessity.detail: Minimum requirements and essentials determine priority; happiness items wait until foundations are stable.
necessity.cue: It helps you sort order, moving truly continuity-impacting items to the front.

lifecycle.answer: At what rhythm does it appear?
lifecycle.detail: Consumables need restocking, durables wait for deals, tools buy once, emotional items treat separately.
lifecycle.cue: All are needs, but restocking, waiting for deals, and one-time purchases lead to completely different next steps.

consumables.title: Restock Rhythm
consumables.detail: It gets used up. The point isn't whether it's worth it, but not letting inventory suddenly hit zero.

durables.title: Long-Term Use
durables.detail: It should accompany you for years. Focus on specs, frequency of use, and long-term return.

tools.title: Buy Once, Done
tools.detail: Low presence in daily life, but missing one will block you. Best to prepare in advance.

emotional.title: Treat Separately
emotional.detail: It doesn't exist by function alone, and shouldn't use the same replacement logic as other items.

attributes.label: Chinese Label
attributes.emptyHint: No attribute definitions in this category yet
attributes.selectPrompt: Select an attribute from the left to view details

hint.kind: Determines which dimension this attribute belongs to. Items use these as dropdown options.
hint.code: Use PascalCase, e.g. Consumable. Cannot be changed after creation.
hint.codeLocked: Code cannot be changed after creation — it is the stable database identifier.
hint.label: The label shown in the UI. Can be changed at any time.
hint.labelEn: Shown when the language is set to English. Falls back to the Chinese label if empty.
hint.semanticStatus: Built-in identifier for owned/wanted logic. Must match the system's business semantics. Locked for system attributes.
hint.semanticChannel: Channel attributes do not support semantic keys. Leave empty.
hint.semantic: Links this attribute to a built-in semantic (optional). Custom attributes usually leave this empty.
hint.styleToken: Badge background: accent=primary · secondary=secondary · muted=muted grey · card=card
hint.rank: Assign an order weight for ranked attributes (e.g. depreciation rate). Lower = lower rank. Leave empty for unordered attributes.
hint.description: Explains the meaning of this attribute. Only shown in the attribute management panel.

attributes.disableDescription: Some items are currently using this attribute.
attributes.disableKeepLegacy: Existing items will still be saved (old value passed through)
attributes.disableShowWarning: Disabled attributes will show a warning in the edit UI
attributes.disableBlockNew: Disabled attributes cannot be selected for new items
attributes.disabledWarning: This status is disabled. Consider selecting a different one.
attributes.conflict: This attribute was changed elsewhere — refreshed to the latest state, please retry.

error.itemRequired: Enter a name and select at least one system and one space
error.semanticRequired: This category requires a semantic key
error.codeFormat: Only letters, digits, and underscores are allowed, e.g. Consumable

validation.priceOrder: Buy-below price cannot be greater than overpay price
validation.duplicateSystem: A stage template cannot contain the same system twice
validation.emptySection: Each system group needs at least one item
validation.duplicateName: That name already exists. Use a different one.
